description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with six stages: go-fmt, go-test, build-src, build-docker, docker-registry-master, and docker-registry-tags. Use golang:1.14 for the go-fmt job (which runs `cd api && test -z $(go fmt)`) and the go-test job (which sets up GOPATH and PATH, links the project into /go/src, then runs `cd /go/src/$CI_PROJECT_NAME && make test`), adding a postgres:9.6 service and defining COMMENTO_POSTGRES, POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_USER, and POSTGRES_PASSWORD variables; both jobs are excluded from master and tag pipelines. Use debian:buster for the build-src job (with a before_script `bash $CI_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/gitlab-ci-build-prescript`, then `export GOPATH=/go`, `export PATH=$PATH:/go/bin`, `cd /go/src/$CI_PROJECT_NAME`, `make devel`, `make prod`) and docker:stable for the build-docker job (which runs `docker build -t commento .` with a docker:dind service), both also excluded from master and tags. Add a docker-registry-master job that runs only on the master branch of commento/commento, logs into registry.gitlab.com with the CI job token, pulls the latest image if it exists, builds the image using `--cache-from` and tags it `registry.gitlab.com/commento/commento:latest`, then pushes it, using docker:stable and docker:dind. Finally, add a docker-registry-tags job that runs only on tags, installs git via `apk add git`, builds the image tagged with `$(git describe --tags)`, and pushes it to the same registry, also using docker:stable and docker:dind.
